0.title: Stage 0: Tutorial - First Circuit
0.desc: Recreate the basic circuit by following the instructed logic path. Invert input IN1, combine it with IN2 using AND, and output to OUT1.

1.title: Stage 1: NOT Gate
1.desc: Build a circuit that inverts the input signal. 0 must output 1, and 1 must output 0.

2.title: Stage 2: OR Gate
2.desc: Design logic that outputs 1 when at least one of the two inputs is 1.

3.title: Stage 3: AND Gate
3.desc: Build a circuit that outputs 1 only when both inputs are 1.

4.title: Stage 4: NOR Gate
4.desc: Implement logic that inverts the result of an OR operation.

5.title: Stage 5: NAND Gate
5.desc: Design a circuit that inverts the result of an AND operation.

6.title: Stage 6: XOR Gate
6.desc: Reconstruct a circuit that outputs 1 only when the two inputs are different.

7.title: Stage 7: Majority Gate
7.desc: Output 1 only when at least two of the three inputs are 1.

8.title: Stage 8: Parity Checker
8.desc: Build a circuit that outputs 1 only when the number of 1s is odd.

9.title: Stage 9: Half Adder
9.desc: Compute the sum of two 1-bit inputs and output Sum (S) and Carry (C) separately.

10.title: Stage 10: Full Adder
10.desc: Design a circuit that adds three 1-bit inputs and outputs Sum (S) and Carry (C).

11.title: Stage 11: 2-to-4 Decoder
11.desc: Configure the circuit so that exactly one of four outputs becomes 1 for each 2-bit input combination.

12.title: Stage 12: 4-to-1 MUX
12.desc: Pass one of four inputs directly to OUT according to select signals (S1 S0).

13.title: Stage 13: 2-bit Comparator
13.desc: Compare 2-bit numbers A and B so that only one of A>B, A=B, or A<B is 1.

14.title: Stage 14: 2-bit Adder
14.desc: Add 2-bit numbers A and B and output the 3-bit result (S2 S1 S0).

15.title: Stage 15: mod 4 2-bit Subtractor
15.desc: Compute A − B for two 2-bit numbers using mod 4 arithmetic and output the 2-bit result.

16.title: Stage 16: 2-bit Max Selector
16.desc: Output the larger value between two 2-bit numbers A and B.

17.title: Stage 17: 2-bit Multiplier
17.desc: Compute the product of two 2-bit numbers and output the 4-bit result (P3 P2 P1 P0).

18.title: Stage 18: Mod 3 Remainder
18.desc: Output the remainder of dividing 4-bit input A by 3 as a 2-bit value (R1 R0).

19.title: Crossroad
19.desc: Design paths so two input signals are delivered to their own outputs accurately without interference.

20.title: Fixed XOR
20.desc: Implement XOR behavior exactly within the fixed input and output structure.

21.title: 3-bit Shifter
21.desc:
Design a circuit that cyclically shifts three signals based on control signal IN4.
IN4 = 0 -> output as-is
IN4 = 1 -> rotate by one position

22.title: Fixed 2-to-4 Decoder
22.desc: Configure the circuit so exactly one output is activated for each input combination.

23.title: Priority Gate
23.desc: Select and output only the single 1 with the highest priority among multiple inputs.

24.title: 2's complement
24.desc: Design a circuit that computes the 2's complement of a 4-bit binary number.

hints.content: Outputs true only when A∨B is true but A∧B is false.
Outputs true only when A and B, B and C, or C and A are true.
The XOR operation works like addition mod 2.
Determine what operations OUT1 and OUT2 perform on IN1 and IN2.
OUT1 = A XOR B, OUT2 = A∧B
Determine what operations OUT1 and OUT2 perform on IN1, IN2, and IN3.
OUT1 = (A∧B)∨(B∧C)∨(C∧A), OUT2 = (A XOR B) XOR C
Try building circuits for A∧B, A∨B, and C instead of A, B, and C.
Combine circuits for OUT1, OUT2, OUT3, and OUT4 into one.
OUT1, OUT2, OUT3, and OUT4 can be expressed as AND operations of IN1, IN2, ¬IN1, and ¬IN2.
